---
title: "Local LeRobot Training"
---

Use this chapter to run LeRobot training without Azure ML or OSMO. Local execution is useful for quick configuration validation, dataset checks, and short baseline experiments before cluster submission.

## Prerequisites

| Component | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Python | 3.11 or later |
| Dependency manager | `uv` configured for this repository |
| Dataset | Local LeRobot-compatible dataset path |
| GPU | Optional for smoke tests, recommended for full runs |

## Local Workflow

1. Set up the Python environment with `uv`.
2. Validate dataset structure and metadata.
3. Run a short LeRobot training job with reduced steps.
4. Review logs and checkpoints before scaling out.

## Example Command

```bash
cd training/il
uv run python src/lerobot/train.py \
	--dataset.path <local-dataset-path> \
	--policy.type act \
	--output.dir <local-output-dir> \
	--max-steps 1000
```

## Validation Checklist

- Training starts without dataset schema errors.
- Loss decreases for initial iterations.
- Checkpoint files are produced in the output directory.
- Run metadata is captured for reproducibility.

## Related Documentation

- [LeRobot Training](lerobot-training.qmd)
- [Inference Guide](../inference/overview.qmd)
- [MLOps Training Lifecycle](../mlops/training-lifecycle.qmd)
